what_it_does: This file is a thin Node.js executable shim that re-invokes the sibling search-conversations.js script with the same command-line arguments, inherits stdio, and exits with the child's exit code.
observations: Uses ESM import syntax (import.meta.url, fileURLToPath, dirname, join).
Spawns the sibling .js file using process.execPath and passes process.argv.slice(2).
Uses stdio: 'inherit' so input/output map directly to the child process.
Exits with the child's exit code, defaulting to 0 if the code is null or undefined.
standouts: It is an extensionless CLI entry point that delegates all behavior to a .js implementation file.
Uses Node's ESM module resolution and the standard __dirname emulation pattern.
conventions: CLI wrapper pattern separating executable entry point from implementation module.
ESM with import.meta.url for path resolution.
Propagates exit codes and arguments transparently.
concerns: No error handling for spawn failures (e.g., missing search-conversations.js).
Depends on the sibling .js file existing in the same directory.
No shebang-related portability notes beyond #!/usr/bin/env node.
notes: A minimal Node CLI launcher that forwards arguments to the real search-conversations.js script and mirrors its I/O and exit status.
questions: Why is the CLI entry point separated from the implementation file?
Is search-conversations.js expected to be ESM or CommonJS?
intention_signals: Provide a clean, executable CLI command that runs the underlying JavaScript implementation without embedding logic in the extensionless file.
contribution: Provides an extensionless, executable CLI entry point that preserves argument forwarding, stdio passthrough, and exit-code propagation to the sibling .js implementation.
summary_for_embedding: This file is an ESM Node.js CLI wrapper that spawns the sibling search-conversations.js with forwarded arguments, inherited stdio, and propagates the child exit code.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to expose a clean, extensionless command-line entry point that delegates execution to the implementation script while preserving user interaction and exit status.
